qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: PKCS7 Verify() accepts signatures backed by untrusted certificates due to nil trust store default
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes an SM9 infinity-point ciphertext forgery vulnerability in sm9.Decrypt/sm9.UnwrapKey due to missing rejection of the point at infinity on C1. This finding is about PKCS7 Verify() skipping certificate chain validation in the pkcs7 package. The vulnerable code, root cause, affected functions, and impact are entirely different.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SM2 key exchange panics on peer public key with off-curve coordinates
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes an SM9 decryption infinity-point forgery where C1 is not checked for the point at infinity, leading to bilinear pairing degeneration and ciphertext forgery. This finding is about SM2 key exchange not validating that peer public key coordinates are on the curve, causing a panic/DoS. Although both involve missing elliptic curve point validation in the same library (gmsm), the protocols (SM9 vs SM2), vulnerable functions (Decrypt/UnwrapKey vs NewKeyExchange/mqv), root causes (infinity-point not rejected vs off-curve coordinates not validated), and impacts (ciphertext forgery vs process crash) are all different.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory describes an SM9 infinity-point ciphertext forgery vulnerability in github.com/emmansun/gmsm, where sm9.Decrypt/sm9.DecryptASN1/sm9.UnwrapKey do not reject C1 as the point at infinity, enabling ciphertext forgery. Neither qualifying finding identifies this vulnerable code or root cause. The pkcs7_verify_skips_chain_validation finding is about PKCS7 certificate chain validation bypass in a different package. The sm2_keyexchange_invalid_point_panic finding involves SM2 key exchange (a different protocol) with off-curve coordinates causing a panic (different root cause and impact).
evidence: Advisory root cause: sm9 decryption does not reject C1 as the point at infinity in sm9.Decrypt/sm9.DecryptASN1/sm9.UnwrapKey

pkcs7_verify_skips_chain_validation: different package (pkcs7), different vulnerable code (VerifyWithChain(nil)), different root cause (nil trust store), different impact (signature forgery via untrusted cert)

sm2_keyexchange_invalid_point_panic: different protocol (SM2 vs SM9), different functions (NewKeyExchange/mqv vs Decrypt/UnwrapKey), different missing check (IsOnCurve vs infinity-point rejection), different impact (panic/DoS vs ciphertext forgery)
